title: promote-cli validates the resolved realpath of the target prefix but passes the literal unresolved prefix to npm i -g --prefix
body: assertSafeTargetPrefix validates the target prefix by realpath of its nearest-existing ancestor against the repo-root realpath; the install passes the literal unresolved prefix to npm i -g --prefix. The validated representation and the acted-upon representation differ. A symlinked path component of prefix re-pointed in the window between the validate call and the npm install — a window that spans the build and pack steps — is resolved by npm at install time but was not present at validation, so the global install can land on a target the validation never approved, including inside the repo. Precondition: a concurrent writer to a component of the resolved path during the run. Class: validated-target diverges from acted-upon-target — a TOCTOU between a realpath-validated check and a literal-path use.
resolved_by: 60613cc (iterate-4: materialize + fully-resolve + re-validate the target prefix); verified VER-055 (criterion 11 passed) + the iterate-5 adversarial probe. The validated-target-diverges-from-acted-upon-target TOCTOU is closed: assertSafeTargetPrefix now mkdir -p materializes the target, realpathSync-resolves it fully, re-runs the repo-containment refusals on that resolved path, and RETURNS the fully-resolved realpath (safePrefix); the install (npm i -g --prefix safePrefix), the binPath verify, and the location logs all consume safePrefix, so no literal unresolved leaf and no symlink component survives to be re-pointed between validation and install. VER-054 had recorded the leaf-segment residual failed for a not-yet-created override target; 60613cc removed that residual.
